channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Device restriction settings for Android in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: On Android Enterprise or Android for Work devices owned by your organization, you can restrict settings on the device using Microsoft Intune. Allow copy and paste, notifications, app permissions, data sharing, password length, sign in failures, use fingerprint to unlock, reuse passwords, and enable bluetooth sharing of work contacts. Configure devices as a dedicated device kiosk to run one app, or multiple apps.

item.title: macOS device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for macOS devices that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Network endpoints for US government deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See the list of US government endpoint URLs that Intune needs and requires. Allow the ports, IP addresses, and endpoint URLs in your proxy server configuration.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for tvOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Apple TV devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for visionOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Apple Vision Pro devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Network endpoints for China deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review China endpoints for Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for Android Developer Integration and Testing Guide - MAM Integration Essentials - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand MAM integration essentials to incorporate Intune mobile app management (MAM) into your Android app.

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for Android Developer Integration and Testing Guide - App Participation Features - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand App participation features when incorporating Intune mobile app management (MAM) into your Android app.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Factory Reset Protection Emails Not Enforced in Intune for Android - Intune
item.description: Describes an issue in which an Android Enterprise device can be activated by using a Google account that isn't included in the "Factory reset protection emails" setting.

item.title: Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, tvOS, visionOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available device actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Device Action: Delete - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to delete devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Intune device compliance, including tenant-wide compliance policy settings and device compliance policies.

item.title: Create device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device compliance policies for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create discovery scripts for custom compliance policy in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create scripts for Linux, macOS, or Windows devices to discover the settings you define as custom compliance settings for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use custom compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage custom compliance settings on managed devices by using JSON files and discovery scripts in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create a JSON file for custom compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create the JSON file that defines custom settings and values for use with device compliance policies in Intune.

item.title: Multiple Managed Accounts for App Protection Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how the Multiple Managed Accounts (MMA) feature in Microsoft Intune MAM enables users to manage more than one work or school account within a single app.

item.title: Set permissions to Managed Home Screen using Android Enterprise OEMConfig - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the Samsung Knox Service Plugin, Zebra OEMConfig Powered by MX, Legacy Zebra OEMConfig, and Honeywell UEMConnect apps to Intune, and use the app schemas to configure permissions for the Managed Home Screen (MHS) app on Android Enterprise devices.

item.title: Troubleshoot Configuration Management bootstrap with modern provisioning - Intune
item.description: Helps you understand and troubleshoot issues that you may encounter when you set up co-management by taking path 2 - Bootstrap the Configuration Manager client with modern provisioning.

item.title: Android Management API for personally owned work profiles - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the transition to Android Management API for personally owned work profile devices, including web-based enrollment, migration steps, and what IT admins need to know.

item.title: Enroll personal devices in Intune with Android Enterprise work profile management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for personal devices and bring-your-own-device scenarios using Android Enterprise work profile management.

item.title: Set up a macOS ADE token in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, renew, and delete Apple enrollment program tokens for macOS automated device enrollment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Audit changes and events in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to review audit logs that record Microsoft Intune activities.

item.title: Deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow the steps to deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Learn about using Intune to manage Microsoft Defender settings on devices that aren't enrolled with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Intune policy to manage Microsoft Defender security settings on devices that aren't enrolled with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune and onboard devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Connect Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to Intune, onboard devices by platform, and configure compliance and Conditional Access policies that use device risk levels to control access to corporate resources.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Licensing Plans and Options - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune licensing options, plans, and the capabilities included with each Intune plan and Microsoft 365 license tier.

item.title: Prepare a Win32 App to Be Uploaded to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to prepare a Win32 app to be uploaded to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about using Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To enhance the security of your organization, set your users to run with standard permissions while Endpoint Privilege Management ensures those users can seamlessly run specified files with elevated rights.

item.title: Step 5 – Enroll devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune.

item.title: Redo Workplace Join for Android Enterprise devices in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to redo Workplace Join (re-WPJ) on an Android Enterprise device that's already enrolled in Intune but needs Azure AD registration completed.

item.title: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate to manage iOS/iPadOS devices with Intune.

item.title: App Protection Policies Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune app protection policies help protect your company data and prevent data loss.

item.title: Configure iOS/iPadOS Enterprise SSO app extension with MDMs - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Microsoft Enterprise single sign-on (SSO) plug-in. Add or create an iOS or iPadOS device configuration profile using the Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug-in app extension in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Windows device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Windows devices using Automatic enrollment, Windows Autopilot, group policy, and co-management enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Enroll devices using a device enrollment manager account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device enrollment manager account to enroll devices in Intune.

item.title: Use Remediations to Detect and Fix Support Issues - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about Remediations in Microsoft Intune, including what Remediations are and view any prerequisites and licensing requirements. Also, learn how to deploy built-in and custom remediation scripts, and learn how to monitor your scripts.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Use SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and assign Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS App Protection Policy Settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes the iOS/iPadOS app protection policy (APP) settings for iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Configure macOS Enterprise SSO app extension with MDMs - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Microsoft Enterprise single sign-on (SSO) app extension plug-in. Add or create an macOS device profile using the SSO app extension in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers.

item.title: Enable Windows diagnostic data and license verification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enable Intune tenant settings to use Windows diagnostic data and verify Windows licenses required for dependent features.

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Operating systems and browsers supported by Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Lists supported device platforms and browsers for Intune device management

item.title: Apple device restriction settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, or create settings on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices to restrict features in Microsoft Intune. Create password requirements, control the locked screen, use built-in apps, add restricted or approved apps, handle bluetooth devices, connect to the cloud for backup and storage, enable kiosk mode, add domains, and control how users interact with the Safari web browser.

item.title: Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To use Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) with Microsoft Intune, configure your on-premises AD domain, create a certification authority, and set up the NDES server to support use of the Certificate Connector.

item.title: Collect Device Hardware Info With the Properties Catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the properties catalog in Microsoft Intune to collect device hardware info like BIOS version, TPM status, and disk details on managed Windows devices. Get deeper visibility into your device inventory and troubleshoot issues.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune to target policies based on device properties like OS version or manufacturer. Learn to create, update, and delete filters for managed devices and apps.

item.title: Manage Apple Volume-Purchased Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to sync apps you bought in volume through Apple Business Manager with Microsoft Intune. Then manage and track these apps on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Add corporate identifiers to Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add corporate identifiers (enrollment method, IMEI, and serial numbers) to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Supported Microsoft Intune Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides lists of support partner and Microsoft apps that are commonly used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Discovered Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand details about the detected apps that Intune found on a device.

item.title: Add Platform SSO policy to ADE Profile on macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a settings catalog platform single sign-on (PSSO) policy to an Automated Device Enrollment (ADE) profile and configure it to run during Setup Assistant with modern authentication on macOS devices.

item.title: Set up enrollment time grouping - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview and setup of the enrollment time grouping feature in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage approved apps for Windows devices with App Control for Business policy and Managed Installers in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use App Control for Business policies and a managed installer to manage which apps are approved to run on Windows devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set the mobile device management authority - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to set the mobile device management authority in Microsoft Intune and some key considerations.

item.title: Using Remote Help on Windows to Assist Authenticated Users - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Remote Help app to provide remote assistance to authenticated users who also run the Remote Help app, and to troubleshoot for frontline workers (FLW).

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Manage Microsoft Edge on iOS and Android With Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune app protection and configuration policies with Microsoft Edge for iOS and Android to ensure corporate websites are always accessed with safeguards in place.

item.title: Security Copilot in Intune features overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Security Copilot in Intune is an AI platform. It can help you create policies, get information about existing policies, and show more details on specific settings, including their impacts on users and devices. You can also use Copilot to troubleshoot device issues and query devices.

item.title: Add Microsoft Store Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about adding Microsoft Store apps to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Win32 App Management in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune. This article provides an overview of the Intune Win32 app delivery and management capabilities.

item.title: Set up Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 1 for deploying or setting up Intune. The starting point is to review supported configurations, sign up for the trial, configure the custom domain name, add users and groups to Intune, assign licenses to users, manage roles, grant admin permissions, and set the MDM authority.

item.title: Enroll Android Enterprise dedicated, fully managed, or corporate-owned work profile devices in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll Android Enterprise dedicated, fully managed, or corporate-owned work profile devices in Intune.

item.title: Use a PKCS certificate profile to provision devices with certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune, work with root certificates and certificate templates, and use device configuration profiles for a PKCS Certificate.

item.title: Microsoft Intune advanced capabilities - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune advanced capabilities deliver advanced endpoint management and security. Learn what they are, which licenses include them, and how to get them.

item.title: Assign device profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to assign device configuration profiles and policies to users and devices. Learn how to exclude groups from a profile assignment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add PowerShell Scripts to Windows Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and run PowerShell scripts, assign the script policy to Microsoft Entra groups, and use reports to monitor the scripts. See the steps to delete scripts you add on Windows devices in Microsoft Intune. Read common issues and resolutions.

item.title: Install the Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to install and configure the unified Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune, which supports SCEP, PKCS, imported PKCS, and certificate revocation.

item.title: Bulk enrollment for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a bulk enrollment package for Microsoft Intune

item.title: Sign Up for Microsoft Intune Free Trial Setup Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sign up for a Microsoft Intune free trial and configure your tenant. Learn the setup process, prerequisites, and how to configure domain names. Start evaluating Intune today.

item.title: Deploy Intune policies to manage Windows LAPS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create policies to manage the Windows Local Administrator Policy Solution (LAPS) on Windows devices that enrolled with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune core concepts - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune works across identities, devices, and apps, and how the three pillars come together to drive access decisions.

item.title: Overview of Windows LAPS with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can use Microsoft Microsoft Intune and application protection policy to manage the local administrator accounts on Windows devices. Through the Windows LAPS CSP, back up accounts and passwords to Microsoft Entra ID, define password requirements, and protect account passwords through scheduled password rotations and manual rotations at need.

item.title: Find the primary user of a Microsoft Intune device. - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find the primary user (or User Device Affinity) of an Intune device.

item.title: Restrict USB Devices Using Settings Catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to restrict USB devices, including thumb drives, flash drives, USB cameras, and more. Use other settings to allow specific USB devices on Windows 10/11 devices.

item.title: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags for distributed IT - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags to filter configuration profiles to specific roles.

item.title: Single sign-on (SSO) for iOS/iPadOS and macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug-in in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers. The Enterprise SSO plug-in is available on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Set up enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS direct enrollment - Apple Configurator-Setup Assistant - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Apple Configurator to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices with Setup Assistant.

item.title: Set up Intune enrollment for Android Enterprise dedicated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure enrollment in Microsoft Intune for Android Enterprise dedicated devices.

item.title: Add Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add apps to Microsoft Intune so you can assign apps to users and devices. Intune supports a wide range of app types.

item.title: Set up enrollment for Android Enterprise fully managed devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up enrollment in Intune for devices using the Android Enterprise fully managed device management solution.

item.title: Migration guide to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deployment guide to set up, onboard, or move to Intune. These steps include moving from partner MDM providers, using co-management, moving from on-premises group policy, and moving from Office 365 device management.

item.title: Tutorial - Use Apple Business to enroll iOS/iPadOS devices in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you'll set up Apple corporate device enrollment features with Intune to enroll iOS/iPadOS devices purchased through Apple Business.

item.title: Windows compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the settings you can use when setting compliance for your Windows, Windows Holographic, and Surface Hub devices in Microsoft Intune. Check for compliance on the minimum and maximum operating system, set password restrictions and length, check for partner anti-virus (AV) solutions, enable encryption on data storage, and more.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure common Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices. You can enable Kerberos SSO to on-premises Active Directory and cloud-based Microsoft Entra ID, use Touch ID biometric policy with Secure Enclave authentication, and enable SSO on non-Microsoft apps. You can also configure end user experience settings.

item.title: Learn about Intune security baselines for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that have preset and recommended configurations to the Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up local admin account creation and password management for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up macOS account configuration with LAPS through automatic device enrollment for macOS devices in Intune.

item.title: Default configuration of Intune's Windows security baselines - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the default setting configuration of the various Microsoft Intune security baselines for Windows.

item.title: Set up the Enrollment Status Page in the admin center copy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up a greeting page for users signing in and enrolling Windows devices copy.

item.title: Create trusted certificate profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and deploy trusted certificate profiles to deploy a trusted root certificate to managed devices in Intune. Trusted certificate profiles support use of Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) and Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.
